source_reviews.notes: The reopened SNAAP page supports the Fall 2025 survey timing, more than 2,000 arts-and-design alumni, 51.7% professional generative-AI use, and reported productivity, idea-development, and editing uses. It supports the stated broad adoption proxy but not a code-specific 541490 establishment rate or an exit multiple, both of which the packet labels as estimates and caveats.

The reopened BLS page supports the cited fashion-designer workflow: trend research, collection themes, CAD, fabric selection, prototypes, presentation, manufacturer interaction, and final-production oversight. It supports the digital-versus-physical task distinction and is not presented as direct evidence for the estimated automation fractions.

The reopened McKinsey page supports that more than 35% of fashion and luxury executives report generative-AI use in selected functions such as image creation and product discovery. The record candidly treats this as broad directional evidence rather than a 541490 studio census.

The reopened Upwork page supports $20-$50 hourly pricing, hourly or milestone billing, project budgets for garments, tech packs, collections, launches, and ongoing support, plus sourcing, manufacturer collaboration, fitting, and production-coordination work. The marketplace-proxy limitation is explicitly authored.

The reopened U.S. Bank release supports the 1,000-owner nationwide sample, two-to-99-employee and up-to-$25M-revenue scope, more-than-half-over-55 context, and 54% succession-plan statistic. The packet uses it only as a disclosed broad owner-age proxy and does not claim a 541490 shortage.

The reopened Vogue page supports that the discussed transactions concern brands, retailers, licensing, scale, and portfolios, alongside conservative valuations and defensive consolidation. The packet correctly uses this as negative boundary evidence and discloses that it is not a 541490 transaction comp or proof of zero private buyers.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 5414 and is contaminated by sibling interior and graphic-design occupations.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fashion is a hypothesis for the dominant 541490 revenue subsegment, not a verified code-level revenue split.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: No source maps AI task exposure directly to the supplied wage shares.
inputs.pi_dist: Marketplace work is a pricing and supply proxy, not a census of U.S. design-studio contracts.
inputs.pi_moat: The assessment is for the hypothesized fashion-design dominant subsegment and does not establish a code-wide moat.
inputs.t50_years: Neither source reports a U.S. 541490 establishment-level adoption rate.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The direct 51.7% observation is not code-specific and includes arts/design alumni outside specialized design services.
inputs.historical_analogs: No 541490 historical price or margin series was located.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: The source covers U.S. firms with two to 99 employees and up to $25M revenue, not 541490 or owners aged 60+ specifically.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not turn generic owner retirement pressure into a target-code roll-up supply claim.
inputs.active_consolidators: Absence of confirmed public evidence is not proof that no private buyer exists.
inputs.buy_mult: No observed 541490 lower-middle-market transaction multiple was found.
inputs.exit_mult: This is a stress assumption, not a direct transaction observation; broad fashion brand M&A is intentionally excluded as a comp.
inputs.pricing_structure: Marketplace prices and billing modes may not represent every U.S. specialized design studio.
summary: Mechanics and exact artifact bindings pass. The reopened sources support the stated fashion-workflow, adoption, pricing, succession, and M&A-boundary facts; the record remains useful because every material code-scope, role-mix, marketplace, adoption, and transaction-evidence limitation is explicitly disclosed and the resulting kill thesis does not depend on hiding those proxies.
